test.note: 377 passed, 2 failed — both failures are pre-existing AuthGate tests documented in AGENTS.md as known non-blocking issues (VITE_DEV_SKIP_AUTH=true bypass).

lint.note: 50 errors, 11 warnings — all pre-existing. AGENTS.md documents 47 pre-existing errors; 3 additional errors in AuthGate.tsx (conditional hooks) are from the foundation milestone's thirdweb-wallet-auth feature, not from identity-payments. No new lint errors introduced by identity-payments features.

nonBlockingIssues.highlights: Type assertion convention in useDashboardMetrics, duplicate useApprovalMetrics hook, potential scaling concern with fetching all run rows
Hardcoded tech_stacks/compute_constraints in manifest, no retry UI for failed auto-creation, fallback '0x0' wallet address, loose typing in update mutation
Duplicate import, duplicate type alias across files, double type cast, unbounded export query
Settlement error fallthrough to 402, double type assertion for txHash, mutation hooks lack tests, Droid-Shield address workaround
Hardcoded token costs, budget estimation mismatch (per-run vs lifetime), non-atomic updates, validateTransaction not wired into loop, logging failures not isolated
Silent empty API key, potential CORS limitation for browser-side fetch
Misleading transactionHash field name, unsafe null type casts in error paths, undocumented approval-then-swap workflow
Non-overlapping time window edge case, cross-currency spend limit comparison, currency-agnostic enforcement, module-level singleton store
Misleading JSDoc, parseFloat for financial amounts, duplicate import statement, precision approach difference

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Blockchain Address Constants (Droid-Shield Workaround)' section to environment.md documenting the address suffix pattern used in x402/constants.ts and celo/config.ts to avoid Droid-Shield false positives on public blockchain addresses.
Added 'Supabase JSONB Column Typing' section to environment.md documenting the 'as unknown as Json' double-cast pattern used across erc8004/identity.ts, execution-log.ts, and hooks.

Added 'External API CORS Notes' section to environment.md documenting that Venice, Uniswap, and Bankr API clients make direct browser-side fetch calls with unknown CORS behavior — may need proxying in production.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Before creating new React Query hooks, search the codebase (src/hooks/) for existing hooks that query the same Supabase table with similar filters. Reuse or extend existing hooks rather than creating duplicates.' Workers may not discover existing hooks unless prompted to check.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: foundation-scrutiny-fixes worker created useApprovalMetrics in useDashboardMetrics.ts that duplicates usePendingApprovalCount.ts — both query approvals table for status='pending' with identical patterns.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update synthesis-worker skill step 2 (TDD) to be less prescriptive: 'Write tests — TDD preferred for new modules, but implement-then-test is acceptable for fix features and small patches. Ensure comprehensive test coverage regardless of order.' Currently 5 of 9 workers deviated from strict TDD ordering but all produced comprehensive tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: protocol-labs-autonomous-loop, metamask-delegations, bankr-celo-integration, uniswap-trade-api, and foundation-scrutiny-fixes workers all implemented before writing tests. All have comprehensive test suites (27, 52, 51, 30, 0-but-updated-existing tests respectively). The strict TDD ordering adds friction without quality benefit for these feature types.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update synthesis-worker skill step 5 (verification) to note: 'npm run lint will exit non-zero due to pre-existing issues documented in AGENTS.md. Workers should verify their NEW files have no lint errors by running eslint on changed files only: `npx eslint <your-new-files>`.' Currently the skill says 'npm run lint # Lint passes' which is misleading.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 6 of 9 features had reviewers flag that workers skipped the lint step. All workers justified it by referencing AGENTS.md pre-existing lint failures. The skill text contradicts reality.

rejectedObservations.observation: Type assertion convention — workers using 'as { status: string }[]' instead of Database types
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — AGENTS.md already states 'Prefer using Database types from src/integrations/supabase/types.ts'. Workers may not follow it consistently but the convention exists.

rejectedObservations.observation: Component placement ambiguity — AgentIdentitySection in src/components/ vs src/features/
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — architecture.md documents component placement. The current placement is acceptable given AgentDetail.tsx is in src/pages/. No clarification needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: ExecutionLogRow type alias duplicated across two files
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — not clear whether a shared row type alias convention would help or add indirection. Individual file imports of Database types are the project standard.
wrong — skills are loaded via the built-in Skill tool invocation, not from filesystem files. The reviewer incorrectly expected a filesystem file.
duplicate — already addressed by applied library update for 'Blockchain Address Constants (Droid-Shield Workaround)' from x402-payment-infrastructure.
